_note: Each entry pairs with the identically-named .png in this folder. 'application' is what you'd type into the form's fields. 'expected' documents what the tool should return, for manual sanity-checking.

case1_obvious_pass.expected: PASS on every field. All values match the label exactly, warning is present with correct wording and formatting.

case2_abv_mismatch.expected: FAIL overall. ABV field FAILs specifically — application says 40%, label says 45%. All other fields PASS.

case3_formatting_near_match.expected: PASS overall. Brand name differs only in casing ('Stone's Throw' on label vs 'STONE'S THROW' in application) — normalization should treat this as an exact match, per Dave's interview note that this shouldn't be flagged as a real mismatch.

case4_missing_warning.expected: FAIL overall. government_warning field FAILs with 'No government warning statement found on label' — all other fields PASS.

case5_warning_formatting_issue.expected: WARNING overall. Warning text wording is correct, but it's rendered as 'Government Warning:' (title case, not bold/all-caps) rather than 'GOVERNMENT WARNING:' — should WARN, not hard FAIL, per Jenny's real example.

case6_missing_field.expected: FAIL overall. net_contents field FAILs with 'Net contents was not found on the label image' — label has no net contents text at all.
